content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 19, 1990


Diane M. Fishburn
OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL,
FONG & LEIDIGH
300 Capitol Mall
Suite 350
Sacramento, CA  95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File Nos. A-90-231, A-90-232
and A-90-233

Dear Ms. Fishburn:

     This is in response to your request for confirmation of a
recent determination by the Commission concerning whether, under
the Political Reform Act (the "Act")¹, candidates may contribute
their campaign funds to ballot measure committees controlled by
other candidates. Your request pertains to the following
candidates and ballot measure committees. [L] Governor Leo
McCarthy and Californians for Safe Streets (Our File No. A-90-
231); Assemblymember Tom Hayden and the California Environmental
Protection Act of 1990, Hayden Committee (Our File No. A-90-232);
and Attorney General John Van de Kamp, Californians for Clearing
Out Drug Dealers, Californians for Cleaning Up Government and
Californians for Cleaning Up the Environment (Our File No. A-90-
233).

     In its Hiltachk Advice Letter, No. A-89-533, the Commission
staff indicated, among other things, that a candidate was
prohibited from contributing his or her campaign funds to a ballot
measure committee controlled by another candidate. The basis for
this advice was Section 85304, which prohibits the transfer of
campaign contributions between candidates.




___________________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Our File Nos. A-90-231, A-90-232 and A-90-233
Page 2


At its meeting on March 13, 1990, the Commission reconsidered
this advice and directed staff to advise that such transfers were
permissible between candidates and ballot measure committees
controlled by other candidates. Accordingly, the Commission staff
has revised the advice given to Mr. Hiltachk.

We enclose for your reference a copy of the letter to Mr.
Hiltachk, dated March 16, 1990, which modifies our original
advice.

Should you have any questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Scott Hallabrin
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:SH:ld

Enclosures

sections.question_synthetic: Whether candidates may contribute their campaign funds to ballot measure committees controlled by other candidates under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Yes, candidates may contribute their campaign funds to ballot measure committees controlled by other candidates. The Commission reversed its prior advice (A-89-533) that had prohibited such transfers based on Section 85304, determining at its March 13, 1990 meeting that such transfers are permissible.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections; question and conclusion synthesized from body text. No section headers present. Three related file numbers (A-90-231, A-90-232, A-90-233) addressed in single letter. OCR errors present in original document but did not impede extraction.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether candidates may contribute their campaign funds to ballot measure committees controlled by other candidates under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: Yes, candidates may contribute their campaign funds to ballot measure committees controlled by other candidates. The Commission reversed its prior advice (A-89-533) that had prohibited such transfers based on Section 85304, determining at its March 13, 1990 meeting that such transfers are permissible.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter confirming that candidates may contribute campaign funds to ballot measure committees controlled by other candidates, reversing prior guidance from Advice Letter A-89-533. The determination addresses requests from Lt. Governor Leo Ucarthy, Assemblymember Tom Hayden, and Attorney General John Van de Kamp regarding contributions to their respective ballot measure committees.
